items.title: Biotech’s China problem: An issue of national security or economic value for the US?
items.snippet: Congressional letters sent to the CEOs of Eli Lilly, Pfizer, Merck, BMS and AbbVie this week voicing concerns about the pharmas' clinical trials in China highlight an ongoing discrepancy in how government and industry think about the rise of the Asian country’s biotech industry.

items.title: 7 FDA decisions to watch for in Q3
items.snippet: Many of the FDA’s decisions this quarter involve applications that have previously been delayed, declined or outright rejected, including one for an mRNA vaccine that became the center of controversy earlier this year.

items.title: AstraZeneca taps China’s CSPC again, betting up to $1.77B to target kidney diseases
items.snippet: AstraZeneca and CSPC Pharmaceutical Group have already inked two other agreements this year, including an obesity-focused deal in January and one focused on chronic diseases in June.

items.title: Merck axes Alzheimer’s study, adding to partner Neuphoria’s mounting woes
items.snippet: The mid-stage disappointment in Alzheimer’s disease delivers another blow to Neuphoria Therapeutics, which in November last year was forced to launch a strategic business review after a Phase 3 trial in social anxiety disorder failed.

items.title: Vertex’s Casgevy becomes first approved gene therapy for young kids with rare blood disorders
items.snippet: The FDA approved the expansion of Casgevy, which had previously been greenlit for patients 12 and up, into a younger pediatric population under the agency’s Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher program.

items.title: The top 12 companies hiring in biopharma now
items.snippet: Looking for a biopharma job? Check out the BioSpace list of 12 top companies hiring life sciences professionals like you.

items.title: 8 companies hiring in Greater Philadelphia
items.snippet: Looking for your next role in Philly? Here are eight companies currently hiring.

items.title: Webinar: Streamlining Nonclinical Development with FDA NAMs
items.snippet: FDA’s draft guidance on the validation of non-animal methods (NAMs) is reshaping how nonclinical programs are designed and justified. This webinar explores how sponsors can apply NAMs to streamline development, improve human relevance, and support IND decision-making within a weight-of-evidence framework.

items.title: Ipsen sends clear Memo to rare diseases with up to $796M buyout
items.snippet: Ipsen is penning its second acquisition of the week, this time securing Memo Therapeutics and its midstage monoclonal antibody in a deal that could approach $800 million.

items.title: Orca opens up Treg cell therapy with FDA nod for allogeneic blood cancer treatment
items.snippet: The approval of Tregzi—the first regulatory greenlight for Orca Bio—was based on a Phase 3 study in which patients on the therapy were twice as likely to survive without cancer relapse and without chronic GVHD compared with conventional allogeneic transplant.

items.title: Eli Lilly and Regeneron among 7 companies picked for FDA PreCheck pilot program
items.snippet: Teams at facilities being developed by Eli Lilly, Regeneron and other companies will receive early technical guidance and additional perks from the FDA.

items.title: Vistagen in freefall as social anxiety drug fails Phase 3
items.snippet: Despite the late-stage fail, Vistagen will nevertheless continue to push its drug candidate forward and meet with the FDA to align on a potential registrational path.

items.title: Sarepta secures early 2027 FDA decision for Duchenne drugs, pushing past confirmatory fail
items.snippet: Sarepta Therapeutics is seeking to convert the accelerated approval of its therapeutic exon-skippers for Duchenne muscular dystrophy to full despite the drugs’ failure to improve motor function in a confirmatory trial.

items.title: Sentiment at BIO soars as FDA resets, dealmaking evolves; Amgen’s Tavneos in hot water
items.snippet: The vibe at BIO 2026 in San Diego last week was overwhelmingly positive, with attendees observing noticeable changes at the FDA and an uptick in dealmaking and IPOs. Plus, a top medical journal this week retracted a pivotal study for Amgen’s rare disease drug Tavneos, which has been in the FDA’s crosshairs since Janua...

items.title: Golden Triangle still dominates, but talent and capital gaps slow biotech growth
items.snippet: The U.K.’s core biotech cluster continues to produce world‑class science, but investors say limited talent mobility, uneven regional growth and tightening early‑ and mid‑stage capital are slowing the country’s ability to scale new companies.

items.title: Opinion: Innovation wins the contract. Complexity loses the renewal
items.snippet: Innovation drives CROs to win new business, but unchecked customization creates complexity that undermines reproducibility, weakens renewals and leads to turnover—making standardization a strategic necessity, not a constraint.

items.title: Opinion: The life sciences executive search crisis: When 'cutting to the bone' backfires
items.snippet: The 2022 search approach is no longer working. Companies need to think critically about how they are acquiring top regulatory, clinical and commercial executives or risk their programs in this new regulatory and market reality.

items.title: Biopharma strikes 50+ M&As deals in H1, led by Lilly’s $25B spend
items.snippet: The total of 52 mergers and acquisitions for the first half of 2026 reflects what analysts, industry watchers and executives are saying over and over: M&A is back.

items.title: Deep dive: Pharma CEO compensation report
items.snippet: Who is the highest paid CEO in all of pharma? In this special edition, BioSpace examines top paid CEOs and their pay packages.

items.title: FDA rejects Unicycive and Sobi filings over contract manufacturing deficiencies
items.snippet: Unicycive Therapeutics and Sobi received complete response letters for kidney disease and gout filings, respectively, after the FDA found fault with their manufacturing partners.

items.title: Biotech’s China problem: An issue of national security or economic value for the US?
items.snippet: Congressional letters sent to the CEOs of Eli Lilly, Pfizer, Merck, BMS and AbbVie this week voicing concerns about the pharmas' clinical trials in China highlight an ongoing discrepancy in how government and industry think about the rise of the Asian country’s biotech industry.

items.title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
items.snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

items.title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
items.snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

items.title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
items.snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.
